courses.title: Mindfulness Based Stress Reduction Course
courses.summary: Earlybird: Use "MBSR" to get 10% off your booking. Offer expires 5th April, 2026. Reduce stress and cultivate inner peace. Stress touches different aspects of our lives—whether it is in work,…
courses.descriptionHtml:
Earlybird: Use "MBSR" to get 10% off your booking. Offer expires 5th April, 2026.


Reduce stress and cultivate inner peace. Stress touches different aspects of our lives—whether it is in work, relationships, financial difficulties, or even daily hassles. And while the challenges of stress are real—the anxiety, the fear, the distress, the worry—there is hope.


With the Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR) programme, we invite you to participate in a transformational 8-week event combining in-person classes and home-guided practice to explore your relationship with stress—what it is, how it impacts you, and how you can cultivate skills to grow from adversity.


Devoting yourself to this programme will be a gift to yourself and others—an enriching, life-changing practice that will guide you into the inner workings of your mind and also transform how you show up for your family, friends and the people around you.


Who should enrol in this programme?


Parents, therapists, leaders, educators, caregivers, and professionals who face daily stress will benefit from this programme.


The MBSR programme offers a holistic approach to managing stress through mindfulness practices.

  1. Guided meditation sessions in class
  2. Home practice (audio and video guides)
  3. Yoga and movement practices
  4. Group discussions and support


Through regular participation in this programme, you will learn how to:

  1. Recognise and manage stress
  2. Develop a daily mindfulness practice
  3. Increase self-awareness
  4. Improve relationships with others
  5. Improve overall well-being and quality of life


Date, Time & Location

  1. Orientation.
  2. In the orientation sessions, we will be introducing MBSR and mindfulness in details. We will also be engaging in some mindfulness practice to prepare you for the course.
  3. 14 Mar Saturday, 3 - 4:30pm 
  4. 3 Apr Good Friday, 1:30 - 3pm 
  5. MBSR Lessons: MBSR course will be held over 8 Saturdays from 2nd May - 20th June, 3:00 - 5:30pm. A recording of the class will be available for one week after each lesson, so you can catch up if you miss it before the next lesson.
  6. Day of Mindfulness
  7. During the Day of Mindfulness on Vesak Day 1st June, 10am - 6pm, we will alternate between sitting and movement-based mindfulness practices. It allows us time and space to fully experience and master the techniques we learnt from the first few lessons.


About the instructor: Loh Wei


Loh Wei's mindfulness teaching is rooted in the understanding that true resilience is cultivated from within. He discovered this firsthand through a dual journey: navigating the pressures of owning an accountancy firm while caring for his father through a long illness. This profound experience of holding two demanding realities together revealed mindfulness not as an escape, but as a powerful way to engage with life’s complexities with clarity and heart.


As a Qualified MBSR Teacher trained at University of California San Diego, he translates this insight into a practical, trauma-sensitive approach. He fosters a safe, supportive space where individuals can explore their own experiences without judgment and see old patterns with fresh eyes. His work is a gentle invitation to move beyond the pressure to fix everything and instead learn to work skilfully with the present moment, cultivating the focus, empathy, and quiet strength for a more sustainable way of living and working.

courses.title: Bhajans & Satsang with Devdas & Kirsten Sahaja
courses.summary: Earlybirds: Use code SAHAJA to get 10% off the registration fee. Offer expires 31st Â. BHAJANS | STORYTELLING | SATSANG If one of your intentions is to bring more joy and presence into your life,…
courses.descriptionHtml:
Earlybirds: Use code SAHAJA to get 10% off the registration fee. Offer expires 31st Â.

BHAJANS | STORYTELLING | SATSANG

If one of your intentions is to bring more joy and presence into your life, this evening is your invitation.

Join us for a soulful night of devotional chanting.

What Are Bhajans?

Bhajans are the practice of Bhakti Yoga—the yoga of devotion through mantra and chanting.

This ancient practice works directly with your energetic and emotional body, creating profound shifts that happen effortlessly:

  1. Freeing the energetic system from stagnation and blockages
  2. Purifying the emotions through the vibration of sacred sound
  3. Transforming joy into bliss through the power of devotion

Through chanting the many names of Krishna, we attune to the frequencies of sweetness, joy, and deep connection—qualities that naturally arise when the heart opens.

No experience necessary. Bhajans are a direct path accessible to everyone, whether you've been chanting for years or this is your first time.

THE EVENING'S JOURNEY

Devotional Chanting

Immerse yourself in the call-and-response tradition of Bhajan singing. Let the mantras carry you beyond the thinking mind into the spaciousness of the heart.

Storytelling

Discover the wisdom teachings embedded in timeless tales that illuminate the path of love, presence, and spiritual awakening.

Satsang with Devdas

Following the chanting, we gather for spiritual dialogue. This is your opportunity to bring forward heartfelt questions about yoga, spirituality, and life—and receive guidance that helps dissolve what stands in the way of deeper seeing.

THIS EVENING IS FOR YOU IF:

✓ You're seeking more joy and presence in your life

✓ You're curious about devotional practices

✓ You want to experience the transformative power of mantra

✓ You're ready to open your heart in community

✓ You have questions about yoga and spiritual life

✓ You're completely new to Bhajans (beginners warmly welcomed!)

WHAT TO EXPECT

This is a participatory evening. You'll be invited to:

  1. Sing along with the chants (lyrics provided)
  2. Let the music move through you
  3. Rest in the vibration of sacred sound
  4. Share in community
  5. Ask questions and receive guidance

Come as you are. There's no special preparation needed—just an open heart and willingness to experience.

YOUR GUIDES: KIRSTEN & DEVDAS

Priyan's Note: "My own yoga journey has been profoundly shaped by these two remarkable teachers. Kirsten and Devdas have been my guiding lights and most cherished mentors, transforming not just my practice but my entire understanding of yoga's depth. Their teachings continue to inspire every class I share with you. This evening truly represents the coming together of two parts of my heart—my teachers and my students—in one sacred space. It means the world to me to share their wisdom with you."

Kirsten and Devdas believe that life is meant to be fulfilling, joyful, and even magical—the same calling that brings us all to our yoga mats. Through their own experience and over two decades of combined practice and teaching, they know that yoga extends far beyond postures to become a profound process of aligning with life's inherent intelligence.

As founders of Sahaja Collective, they are dedicated to reviving essential teachings on breath as the key that unlocks yoga's extraordinary potential. Their approach harmonizes the energetic system, allowing the body, nervous system, and emotional layers to naturally shift into greater well-being.

Having guided thousands of students worldwide through in-person and online teachings, they bring authentic wisdom, warmth, and accessibility to every gathering. This extraordinarily rare visit to Singapore offers our community a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to experience the teachings that form the foundation of our school's approach.

Don't miss this opportunity to awaken your heart through the ancient practice of devotional chanting.

Come sing. Come feel. Come home to the Heart within.

courses.title: 5D4N Renewal: Ayurvedic Yoga Retreat in Ubud
courses.summary: Yoga, Ayurveda & Renewal in Ubud A retreat organised by Being Sattvaa · Led by Priyan Nithya 25–29 June 2026 · Ubud, Bali Ubud is the yogic heart of Bali — and Being Sattvaa is where that heart beats…
courses.descriptionHtml:
Yoga, Ayurveda & Renewal in Ubud

A retreat organised by Being Sattvaa · Led by Priyan Nithya

25–29 June 2026 · Ubud, Bali


Ubud is the yogic heart of Bali — and Being Sattvaa is where that heart beats most clearly. The resort isn't just a beautiful space; its philosophy is shaped by yogic and Ayurvedic principles from the ground up. Our favourite place to return to.


For five days, step away from the pace of daily life and into something that nourishes and restores you from the inside out. Balance your Dosha and restore your constitution, allowing your body and mind a much needed detox.


Programme


Yoga & Meditation

Twice daily with Priyan — a morning practice to ground you, an evening session to integrate.


Ayurvedic Therapies (3 sessions)

Shirodhara · Steam Bath · Marma Poultice & Massage — classical treatments, individually administered.


Knowledge & Consultation

An Ayurveda lecture and private consultation with the resident doctor.


Day Experience

A Balinese water purification ritual, followed by time in Ubud town.


What's Included

Accommodation · All meals (Ayurvedic vegetarian; eggs available on request) · All therapy sessions · Yoga & meditation · Water purification ritual · Airport transfers


Not included: flights


Investment


Shared room: $2,288

Single occupancy: $2,788


🌿 *Early bird: $200 off when you register before end of May*


This retreat is organised and sold by Being Sattvaa. Bookings and payments are handled directly by them. To enquire or register, email namaste@priyan.yoga and we'll connect you.

courses.title: Ashtanga Mysore with Ken Harakuma
courses.summary: Experience the magic of traditional Mysore-style practice with visiting Master Ken Harakuma, Japan's pioneering Ashtanga teacher directly authorized by Sri K. Pattabhi Jois. This self-paced morning…
courses.descriptionHtml:
Experience the magic of traditional Mysore-style practice with visiting Master Ken Harakuma, Japan's pioneering Ashtanga teacher directly authorized by Sri K. Pattabhi Jois.


This self-paced morning session offers personalized guidance in a group setting. Ken will provide hands-on adjustments and individual attention as you move through your practice at your own rhythm. Whether you are working through the early postures of Primary Series or deepening an established practice, Ken meets you exactly where you are.


With over three decades of teaching experience and a lifetime devoted to the Ashtanga tradition, Ken's gentle yet precise approach makes this an unmissable opportunity for sincere practitioners at every level.


Suitable for: All levels — from those new to Mysore-style practice to experienced Ashtanga practitioners.


About Ken Harakuma

Ken Harakuma is the Director of International Yoga Center (IYC) in Tokyo and Japan's first Ashtanga teacher authorized directly by Sri K. Pattabhi Jois. Author of numerous books including Yoga Life and Spirits of The Ashtanga Yoga, Ken has spent over 30 years bringing the depth of the yoga tradition to practitioners across Asia and beyond. He returns to Nithya Priyan School of Yoga for a second year of workshops in Singapore.


Earlybird Offer:

Use code KEN to get 10% off your enrollment fee if you secure your spot before 14 June. Attend both sessions to get a further $8 off.

courses.title: Ashtanga for Beginners w Ken Harakuma
courses.summary: New to Ashtanga or curious about this powerful practice? This workshop with Ken Harakuma offers a grounded, accessible introduction to the Ashtanga Yoga method — its breath, movement, and sequencing…
courses.descriptionHtml:
New to Ashtanga or curious about this powerful practice? This workshop with Ken Harakuma offers a grounded, accessible introduction to the Ashtanga Yoga method — its breath, movement, and sequencing — so you can begin your practice with confidence and safety.


Ken will guide you through the foundational elements of Ashtanga Yoga: the breathing technique (ujjayi pranayama), the linking of breath and movement (vinyasa), and key postures from the opening sequence. Rather than rushing through a full series, this session focuses on building a safe, sustainable foundation that honours the body you practise in today.

Coming from one of the world's most experienced Ashtanga teachers, this is a rare chance to learn the basics directly from someone who has devoted his life to this tradition — with patience, precision, and warmth.


Suitable for: Beginners and those with limited Ashtanga experience. Also valuable for experienced practitioners wanting to revisit fundamentals with fresh eyes.


About Ken Harakuma


Ken Harakuma is the Director of International Yoga Center (IYC) in Tokyo and Japan's first Ashtanga teacher authorized directly by Sri K. Pattabhi Jois. Author of numerous books including Yoga Life and Spirits of The Ashtanga Yoga, Ken has spent over 30 years bringing the depth of the yoga tradition to practitioners across Asia and beyond. He returns to Nithya Priyan School of Yoga for a second year of workshops in Singapore.


Earlybird Offer:

Use code KEN to get 10% off your enrollment fee if you secure your spot before 14 June.

courses.title: Pranayama Intensive with Ken Harakuma
courses.summary: Explore the transformative practice of pranayama — yogic breathing — in this focused intensive with Ken Harakuma. Breath is the bridge between body and mind, and this session offers a rare…
courses.descriptionHtml:
Explore the transformative practice of pranayama — yogic breathing — in this focused intensive with Ken Harakuma. Breath is the bridge between body and mind, and this session offers a rare opportunity to study its techniques with depth and care under one of Asia's most experienced yoga teachers.


Ken will introduce key pranayama practices from the classical tradition, guiding you through techniques that calm the nervous system, sharpen mental clarity, and deepen your overall yoga practice. Expect instruction on breath awareness, ratio breathing, and foundational pranayama methods including nadi shodhana (alternate nostril breathing) and other traditional techniques.


Whether pranayama is new to you or a regular part of your practice, Ken's decades of experience allow him to teach with a clarity and precision that makes this accessible and deeply rewarding for all.


Suitable for: All levels. No prior pranayama experience necessary.


About Ken Harakuma

Ken Harakuma is the Director of International Yoga Center (IYC) in Tokyo and Japan's first Ashtanga teacher authorized directly by Sri K. Pattabhi Jois. Author of numerous books including Yoga Life and Spirits of The Ashtanga Yoga, Ken has spent over 30 years bringing the depth of the yoga tradition to practitioners across Asia and beyond. He returns to Nithya Priyan School of Yoga for a second year of workshops in Singapore.


Earlybird Offer:

Use code KEN to get 10% off your enrollment fee if you secure your spot before 14 June.

courses.title: Hatha Yoga Adjustment Clinic with Ken Harakuma
courses.summary: Deepen your understanding of yoga asana through the art of adjustment in this hands-on clinic with Ken Harakuma. Whether you are a teacher looking to refine your skills or a dedicated practitioner…
courses.descriptionHtml:
Deepen your understanding of yoga asana through the art of adjustment in this hands-on clinic with Ken Harakuma. Whether you are a teacher looking to refine your skills or a dedicated practitioner wanting to understand alignment more deeply, this workshop offers practical, embodied learning.


Ken will teach adjustment techniques for fundamental postures — demonstrating how to support a student (or your own body) with precision, safety, and sensitivity. You will learn the principles behind effective adjustments: where to place your hands, how to read the body, and when to guide versus when to let be. The session covers key postures from standing, seated, and supine positions, drawing from Ken's integrative background across Ashtanga, Iyengar, and traditional Hatha Yoga.


This clinic balances theory with practice — expect to receive direct feedback from Ken throughout.


Suitable for: All levels. Especially valuable for yoga teachers, teacher trainees, and serious practitioners. No teaching experience required.


About Ken Harakuma

Ken Harakuma is the Director of International Yoga Center (IYC) in Tokyo and Japan's first Ashtanga teacher authorized directly by Sri K. Pattabhi Jois. Author of numerous books including Yoga Life and Spirits of The Ashtanga Yoga, Ken has spent over 30 years bringing the depth of the yoga tradition to practitioners across Asia and beyond. He returns to Nithya Priyan School of Yoga for a second year of workshops in Singapore.


Earlybird Offer:

Use code KEN to get 10% off your enrollment fee if you secure your spot before 14 June.

courses.title: Ashtanga Primary Led with Ken Harakuma
courses.summary: Join Ken Harakuma for a full led Primary Series class — the traditional count-based format where the entire room moves and breathes as one. In a led class, the teacher calls the Sanskrit count for…
courses.descriptionHtml:
Join Ken Harakuma for a full led Primary Series class — the traditional count-based format where the entire room moves and breathes as one.


In a led class, the teacher calls the Sanskrit count for each posture, setting the rhythm of breath and movement for the group. This format builds discipline, stamina, and a deep connection to the practice as it was traditionally taught. Ken's led classes carry a particular energy — precise yet unhurried, demanding yet kind — shaped by decades of study and teaching.


Whether you practise Primary Series regularly or are looking to experience the full sequence under expert guidance, this is a powerful way to immerse yourself in the Ashtanga tradition.


Suitable for: All levels. Familiarity with the Primary Series postures is helpful but not essential — Ken's clear instruction supports practitioners at every stage.


About Ken Harakuma

Ken Harakuma is the Director of International Yoga Center (IYC) in Tokyo and Japan's first Ashtanga teacher authorized directly by Sri K. Pattabhi Jois. Author of numerous books including Yoga Life and Spirits of The Ashtanga Yoga, Ken has spent over 30 years bringing the depth of the yoga tradition to practitioners across Asia and beyond. He returns to Nithya Priyan School of Yoga for a second year of workshops in Singapore.


Earlybird Offer:

Use code KEN to get 10% off your enrollment fee if you secure your spot before 14 June.

courses.title: Restorative & Meditation w Ken Harakuma
courses.summary: Close the weekend with a deeply nourishing session of restorative yoga and meditation with Ken Harakuma. After two days of dynamic practice, this final workshop invites you to slow down, turn inward,…
courses.descriptionHtml:
Close the weekend with a deeply nourishing session of restorative yoga and meditation with Ken Harakuma. After two days of dynamic practice, this final workshop invites you to slow down, turn inward, and experience the quieter dimensions of yoga.


Drawing from his extensive background in healing arts, traditional Hatha Yoga, and meditation, Ken will guide you through a sequence of supported restorative postures designed to release tension and settle the nervous system, followed by a guided meditation practice. This session brings awareness to body, breath, and mind — creating space for stillness and renewal.


Ken's approach to restorative work reflects his belief that yoga is ultimately about living more mindfully and joyfully. This is not passive relaxation — it is a conscious practice of deep rest.


Suitable for: All levels. An ideal complement to the more dynamic workshops, and equally rewarding as a standalone session. No meditation experience necessary.


About Ken Harakuma

Ken Harakuma is the Director of International Yoga Center (IYC) in Tokyo and Japan's first Ashtanga teacher authorized directly by Sri K. Pattabhi Jois. Author of numerous books including Yoga Life and Spirits of The Ashtanga Yoga, Ken has spent over 30 years bringing the depth of the yoga tradition to practitioners across Asia and beyond. He returns to Nithya Priyan School of Yoga for a second year of workshops in Singapore.


Earlybird Offer:

Use code KEN to get 10% off your enrollment fee if you secure your spot before 14 June.

courses.title: The Living Sutras - Three Weekend Intensive
courses.summary: The Living Sutras: A Three-Weekend Experiential Journey The Yoga Sutras of Patanjali contain timeless wisdom about finding peace, clarity, and freedom in daily life. It teachings offer practical…
courses.descriptionHtml:
The Living Sutras: A Three-Weekend Experiential Journey

The Yoga Sutras of Patanjali contain timeless wisdom about finding peace, clarity, and freedom in daily life. It teachings offer practical guidance for navigating stress, relationships, and personal growth with greater awareness and ease.

This welcoming three-weekend course brings the sutras to life through hands-on exploration, meaningful discussion, and gentle inquiry. Whether you're completely new to the Yoga Sutras or looking to deepen your understanding, you'll discover how these ancient insights can become practical tools for modern living.

Drop in for a single session, or attend all six for a full comprehensive journey. No prior knowledge required - just curiosity and openness to explore. Through movement, breath, reflection, and community sharing, you'll find your own way into this profound wisdom tradition.

Course Structure

Each weekend follows a carefully designed rhythm that moves from embodied exploration to conceptual understanding to personal integration:

Saturdays:

  1. 13:00: Gentle and Energising Yoga, Breath-work and Meditation Session
  2. 15:00: Break
  3. 15:30: Lecture and philosophical deepening
  4. 17:00: Integration circle and closing practice
  5. 18:00: End

Sundays:

  1. 13:00: Gentle and Energising Yoga, Breath-work and Meditation Session
  2. 15:00: Break
  3. 15:30: Lecture and philosophical deepening
  4. 17:00: Integration circle and closing practice
  5. 18:00: End

Learning Approach

  1. Embodied Exploration: Each theme begins with movement, breathing, and reflection practices that illuminate the day's focus. These practices are designed to help you discover the wisdom through direct experience.
  2. Philosophical Context: Engaging talks provide background and context, connecting your personal discoveries to the broader wisdom tradition in meaningful ways.
  3. Community Dialogue: Sharing and discussion happen in a supportive environment where diverse perspectives enrich the learning for everyone.
  4. Integration Practice: Between weekends, you'll have opportunities to explore these ideas in your daily life through simple practices, reflections and assignments.

Who Will Enjoy This Course

This course welcomes:

  1. Anyone curious about the philosophical foundations of yoga
  2. People interested in practical wisdom for working with life's challenges
  3. Those drawn to explore consciousness, awareness, and personal growth
  4. Anyone seeking meaningful community around timeless wisdom
  5. Yoga practitioners and teachers seeking deeper understanding (For 200-Hour TTC graduates, it counts as 50 hours towards either your Continuing Education credits or your 300-Hour Certification).

The course is designed to meet participants wherever they are in their journey, offering both introduction to new concepts and fresh perspectives on familiar territory.

Join Us

The Yoga Sutras have guided people toward greater peace and clarity for thousands of years. Come discover how these timeless teachings can support your own journey toward a more conscious, balanced, and fulfilling life.

courses.title: Meditation Weekend Retreat
courses.summary: Spending time to immerse yourself in an intensive meditation retreat offers a multitude of benefits for individuals seeking inner peace and self-discovery. It provides a much-needed respite from the…
courses.descriptionHtml:
Spending time to immerse yourself in an intensive meditation retreat offers a multitude of benefits for individuals seeking inner peace and self-discovery. It provides a much-needed respite from the demands of daily life, allowing participants to detach from distractions and fully immerse themselves in the practice of meditation, which promotes deep relaxation, stress reduction, and mental clarity.


For yogis whose commitments and responsibilities prevent them from embarking on overseas trips or participating in residential yoga retreats, or just need a simple weekend to recharge, we are thrilled to announce our upcoming Weekend Meditation Intensive, right here at the Nithya Priyan School of Yoga.


Our Weekend Intensive will provide a unique opportunity for both beginners and experienced practitioners to come together within the calming environment of our school and immerse themselves in Silence and Stillness. This intensive has been specially designed to provide the same enriching experience that one would typically seek from international or residential retreats.


To book your spot, please contact us or click the signup button. Limited spaces are available as we aim to maintain a small group setting, ensuring personal attention and guidance throughout the weekend.


(Click here if you wish to join the three weekend Meditation Teacher Training Course)


WHAT TO EXPECT


During this Weekend Intensive, you can look forward to:


Daily Yoga Sessions: Delve into rejuvenating and gentle yoga to start each session, providing space and ease within the body and the breath to explore stillness. Classes will cater to practitioners of all levels.


Meditation and Mindfulness: Nurture your mind, body, and spirit. Develop inner peace, enhance focus, and cultivate self-awareness amidst our tranquil environment.


Lectures and Immersive Experiences: These delve deeper into specific aspects of yoga philosophy, neuroscience and physiology that relate to your inner journey.


SATURDAY


13:00 - 15:00 Practice

15:00 - 15:30 Break

15:30 - 18:00 Afternoon Lectures & Practice

18:00 Ends


SUNDAY


13:00 - 15:00 Practice

15:00 - 15:30 Break

15:30 - 18:00 Afternoon Lectures & Practice

18:00 Ends

courses.title: Meditation Teacher Training Course
courses.summary: EMPOWERING YOGIS TO BE SOURCES OF WISDOM AND LIGHT We are excited to announce our upcoming Meditation Teacher Training course, which not only aims to empower individuals with the means of…
courses.descriptionHtml:
EMPOWERING YOGIS TO BE SOURCES OF WISDOM AND LIGHT


We are excited to announce our upcoming Meditation Teacher Training course, which not only aims to empower individuals with the means of transmitting their experiences to others but also emphasizes the importance of giving back to the community. As we embark on this training course, we recognize the significance of giving as we receive. Our intention is to create a community of compassionate and empowered practitioners who can share the gift of meditation with others, spreading peace, wisdom, and light.


Through this 50 Hour Meditation Teacher Training, you’ll be equipped with the knowledge and skills necessary to guide others on their own meditation journeys.


Beyond the technical aspects, this course will emphasise the importance of cultivating a compassionate heart and a deep understanding of the human experience. As meditation practitioners, we recognize that everyone's journey is unique, and our role as teachers is to hold space for others without judgment or attachment. Through experiential learning, discussions, and practical teaching exercises, you’ll develop the capacity to create a safe and inclusive environment for your future students.


The aim is to create a ripple effect where the calm and clarity you experience can positively impact your families, friends, workplaces, and society.


This Teacher Training is not just a certification course; it is an invitation to transcend the personal realm and extend the benefits of meditation to others. By giving back to the community, you become an integral part of a larger movement to create a more harmonious and compassionate world.


We invite all aspiring meditation teachers, seasoned practitioners, and individuals who wish to deepen their practice to join us on this transformative journey. Let us come together, learn, practice, and ultimately empower ourselves.


TECHNIQUES EXPLORED


- Gazing (Trataka)

- Stillness (Kaya Sthairyam)

- Breath Awareness (Anapana Sati)

- Loving Kindness (Metta Bhavana)

- Body Scanning


OPPORTUNITIES FOR PRACTICE


- A half hour mock class conducted during the course with feedback

- Assignment during the week for continuity of your practice

- Invitation to conduct a livestreamed half-hour community meditation class

- If you have a 200 Hours Yoga Teacher Training Completion Certificate, you’ll be eligible to apply to become a teacher for the next term of our Term Teaching Program.


UPON GRADUATION, YOU WILL RECEIVE


- A Completion Certificate

- If you have a 200 Hours Yoga Teacher Training Completion Certificate, your course is claimable for 50 Hours of Continual Education Credits with the Yoga Alliance.

- Alternately, you can count the hours of the course towards your 300 Hour Certification with us, by continuing to take course modules to fulfill the full 300 hours.


TIMETABLE


SATURDAYS


13:00 - 15:00 Afternoon Practice

15:00 - 15:30 Break

15:30 - 18:00 Afternoon Lectures & Practice

18:00 Ends


SUNDAYS

13:00 - 15:00 Afternoon Practice

15:00 - 15:30 Break

15:30 - 18:00 Afternoon Lectures & Practice

18:00 Ends

courses.title: Joy in the Body - THE HIPS Part 1
courses.summary: ✨ Early Bird Bonus: Get 10% OFF when you book early! Use code: HIPS (Expires 31 July 2026) Foundation, Freedom, and Functional Movement "Happy hips" are about more than just flexibility—they are the…
courses.descriptionHtml:
✨ Early Bird Bonus: Get 10% OFF when you book early! Use code: HIPS (Expires 31 July 2026)


Foundation, Freedom, and Functional Movement


"Happy hips" are about more than just flexibility—they are the result of a harmonious relationship between stability and mobility. The strength and range of motion of your ankles, knees, and hips are foundational to safe, healthy, and functional movement, both on and off the mat.


In this intensive, we look at the lower body as an integrated whole. Supplement your physical practice with anatomical lectures, pose breakdowns, and challenging sequences. We will move beyond passive stretching to explore how strong muscles and ligaments create the stability necessary for true openness.


What You Will Learn


This module delves deeply into the biomechanics of the lower body. We will explore the "Joy in the Body" methodology to understand how to protect the knees and ankles, while opening the hips with stable pelvic joints.


The Curriculum Includes:


  1. The Kinetic Chain: Understanding the critical link between ankle mobility, knee safety, and hip mechanics.
  2. Advanced Stretching Techniques: Lectures and application of Reciprocal Inhibition, PNF (Proprioceptive Neuromuscular Facilitation), and Kinesiological stretching to safely increase range of motion.
  3. Structural Stability: The role of strong muscles and ligaments in pelvic stability.
  4. Hands-On Assists: How to safely guide students deeper into hip openers.


Who Is This For?


  1. Practitioners: Ideal for anyone with tight or painful hips, or those seeking to master poses like Lotus, Pigeon, or Squats safely.
  2. Teacher Graduates: For graduates of a 200-Hour TTC, these modules counts towards your Continuing Education (YACEP) credits or your 300-Hour Advanced Certification. 
  3. Each part counts as 26 hours.
  4. Teacher Graduates seeking credit hours are required to join earlier at 10:00 AM and complete additional assignments to fulfill the hours requirement per module.


Schedule & Modules


General Timing: Saturdays & Sundays, [Time e.g., 1:00 PM – 6:00 PM] (Note: To fulfill the 26-hour credit requirement per module, this course includes specific Weekday Assignments and exploration tasks to be completed between weekend sessions).


Part 1: Basic / Essential Level Poses

Dates: 15-22 August 2026

Part 1 covers the foundational movements of the lower body. We focus on proper alignment of the ankles and knees to access the hips safely. This module is essential for understanding the mechanics of standing poses and basic seated forward folds.


Part 2: Essential / Intermediate Level Poses

Dates: 23-30 August 2026

Building on the stability created in Part 1, we move into deeper territory. Part 2 explores complex hip rotations and deep openers, utilizing the PNF and kinesiological techniques learned to expand your boundaries safely.


Course Materials & Investment


Required Text: The Joy in the Body book is required for this course.

Existing Owners: Please bring your copy.

Purchase: Copies available at the studio for a special student price of $30 (U.P. $88).

Tuition:

  1. Single Day Drop-in: $108
  2. Full Module (Part 1 or Part 2): $298 or 19 Class Credits

courses.title: Joy in the Body - THE HIPS Part 2
courses.summary: ✨ Early Bird Bonus: Get 10% OFF when you book early! Use code: HIPS (Expires 31 July 2026) Foundation, Freedom, and Functional Movement "Happy hips" are about more than just flexibility—they are the…
courses.descriptionHtml:
✨ Early Bird Bonus: Get 10% OFF when you book early! Use code: HIPS (Expires 31 July 2026)


Foundation, Freedom, and Functional Movement


"Happy hips" are about more than just flexibility—they are the result of a harmonious relationship between stability and mobility. The strength and range of motion of your ankles, knees, and hips are foundational to safe, healthy, and functional movement, both on and off the mat.


In this intensive, we look at the lower body as an integrated whole. Supplement your physical practice with anatomical lectures, pose breakdowns, and challenging sequences. We will move beyond passive stretching to explore how strong muscles and ligaments create the stability necessary for true openness.


What You Will Learn


This module delves deeply into the biomechanics of the lower body. We will explore the "Joy in the Body" methodology to understand how to protect the knees and ankles, while opening the hips with stable pelvic joints.


The Curriculum Includes:


  1. The Kinetic Chain: Understanding the critical link between ankle mobility, knee safety, and hip mechanics.
  2. Advanced Stretching Techniques: Lectures and application of Reciprocal Inhibition, PNF (Proprioceptive Neuromuscular Facilitation), and Kinesiological stretching to safely increase range of motion.
  3. Structural Stability: The role of strong muscles and ligaments in pelvic stability.
  4. Hands-On Assists: How to safely guide students deeper into hip openers.


Who Is This For?


  1. Practitioners: Ideal for anyone with tight or painful hips, or those seeking to master poses like Lotus, Pigeon, or Squats safely.
  2. Teacher Graduates: For graduates of a 200-Hour TTC, these modules counts towards your Continuing Education (YACEP) credits or your 300-Hour Advanced Certification. 
  3. Each part counts as 26 hours.
  4. Teacher Graduates seeking credit hours are required to join earlier at 10:00 AM and complete additional assignments to fulfill the hours requirement per module.


Schedule & Modules


General Timing: Saturdays & Sundays, [Time e.g., 1:00 PM – 6:00 PM] (Note: To fulfill the 26-hour credit requirement per module, this course includes specific Weekday Assignments and exploration tasks to be completed between weekend sessions).


Part 1: Basic / Essential Level Poses

Dates: 15-22 August 2026

Part 1 covers the foundational movements of the lower body. We focus on proper alignment of the ankles and knees to access the hips safely. This module is essential for understanding the mechanics of standing poses and basic seated forward folds.


Part 2: Essential / Intermediate Level Poses

Dates: 23-30 August 2026

Building on the stability created in Part 1, we move into deeper territory. Part 2 explores complex hip rotations and deep openers, utilizing the PNF and kinesiological techniques learned to expand your boundaries safely.


Course Materials & Investment


Required Text: The Joy in the Body book is required for this course.

Existing Owners: Please bring your copy.

Purchase: Copies available at the studio for a special student price of $30 (U.P. $88).

Tuition:

  1. Single Day Drop-in: $108
  2. Full Module (Part 1 or Part 2): $298 or 19 Class Credits

courses.title: Yoga Foundations
courses.summary: Early Bird Special: Use code "YF4" for 10% off your registration before August 16th. The Beginner's Framework for an Integral Practice Like many others, you probably began your yoga journey in a…
courses.descriptionHtml:
Early Bird Special: Use code "YF4" for 10% off your registration before August 16th.

The Beginner's Framework for an Integral Practice

Like many others, you probably began your yoga journey in a public class. These classes offer an initial glimpse into yoga's transformative potential, introducing us not only to its physical benefits but also to moments of profound peace, clarity, and connection. While these experiences reveal that yoga offers something deeper, regular classes can only take us so far.

An integrated practice invites you beyond the confines of the public class to delve into Alignment, Energy Channels, Philosophy, Nutrition, and Yoga Ethics. This lead you to understand how each element of Yoga interconnects and supports the others. When these components come together, yoga transforms from a mere physical exercise into a comprehensive system for well-being.

Designed for both Beginners and Beginners at Heart, this immersive program offers the comprehensive understanding that regular classes often lack, empowering you to cultivate an integrated practice that serves you both on and off the mat.

A Three Weekend Yoga Immersion

Discover a transformative approach to yoga that goes beyond the basics - a comprehensive framework designed to deepen your understanding of yoga as a holistic practice. Here's why this matters:

  1. Mastering proper Alignment principles ensures every movement is both safer and more powerful.
  2. Comprehending basic Anatomy empowers you to adapt any pose to suit your body's unique needs.
  3. By understanding how Breath influences the nervous system, each pose becomes a powerful tool for mindful movement.
  4. Embracing the Yogic lifestyle beyond the mat with Yogic Nutrition, Purification practices, and Yoga’s Ethical Guidelines fosters mental clarity and resilience.
  5. Grasping fundamental Yoga Philosophy imbues your practice with purpose and direction.

The Perfect Foundation, No Matter Where You Are

Whether you're just starting your yoga journey or have been practicing for years, this immersive format offers unique advantages that public classes cannot provide:

For Beginners:

  1. Start right with proper foundation and alignment
  2. Understand the "why" behind each aspect of your practice from day one
  3. Build confidence through comprehensive understanding
  4. Avoid developing common misconceptions or harmful habits
  5. Get personalized attention to ensure proper form

For Experienced Practitioners:

  1. Fill in the gaps that regular public classes have missed
  2. Deepen your understanding of familiar practices
  3. Learn how to advance your practice safely
  4. Discover the subtle aspects often overlooked in regular classes
  5. Connect isolated pieces of knowledge into a coherent whole

Everyone Benefits From:

  1. Intensive learning format that allows real understanding to blossom
  2. Immediate implementation of concepts
  3. Community support and shared learning
  4. Time to ask questions and explore topics fully
  5. Take-home materials for continued practice

What Makes this Foundations Course Different?

While drop-in classes focus solely on physical postures, Yoga Foundations offers a complete understanding of yoga as a holistic practice. This is the education you've been missing.

Unlike traditional classes, we:

  1. Break down complex biomechanics into clear, applicable principles
  2. Teach you to read your own body and make intelligent adjustments
  3. Build strength and stability where it matters most
  4. Connect ancient wisdom with modern movement science
  5. Provide personalized feedback on your unique practice
  6. Explore yoga philosophy in practical, applicable ways
  7. Study anatomy and the subtle energy body
  8. Learn yogic nutrition principles for enhanced practice
  9. Understand how all aspects of yoga work together

The Intensive Weekend Format

- Saturdays 10am-7pm including lunch break

- Sundays 1pm-7pm

Our three-weekend structure is specifically designed to create lasting transformation:

Immersive Learning Environment:

  1. Dive deep without daily life distractions
  2. Build momentum through consecutive learning days
  3. Experience immediate implementation of concepts
  4. Create lasting connections with fellow students
  5. Develop a strong practice foundation in just three weekends

Each Day is Thoughtfully Structured:

  1. Morning sessions focus on learning and practice
  2. Afternoon sessions blend theory and application
  3. Plenty of breaks for integration and questions
  4. End-of-day integration sessions

Between Weekends:

  1. Take-home practices to explore concepts
  2. Online community support
  3. Access to teachers for questions
  4. Recorded lectures to deepen understanding at your own pace
  5. Time to integrate learnings into daily life

Still Have Questions?

Contact us to discuss if this journey is right for you.

courses.title: Silence: 5D4N Meditation Retreat in Chiang Rai
courses.summary: To signup for this course, please head over to Museflower Retreat to make your reservation and booking. Embark on a transformative journey with our 5D4N Yoga and Meditation Retreat at the Museflower…
courses.descriptionHtml:
To signup for this course, please head over to Museflower Retreat to make your reservation and booking.


Embark on a transformative journey with our 5D4N Yoga and Meditation Retreat at the Museflower Retreat in Chiang Rai. This immersive experience is designed to guide you towards inner peace, deep mindfulness, and profound self-discovery through the powerful practice of noble silence and meditative exploration.


WHO IS THIS FOR:

- Individuals seeking mental clarity and emotional reset

- Meditation practitioners of all levels wanting to deepen their practice

- Professionals experiencing burnout and seeking holistic rejuvenation

- Those curious about mindfulness and inner exploration

- Individuals looking to develop greater self-awareness and emotional intelligence


WHAT TO EXPECT

- Three full days of complete silence and digital detox to facilitate deep introspection

- Guided meditation sessions

- Mindful movement and gentle yoga practices for all levels of proficiency


Growth and Learning:

For Certified Yoga teachers, accumulate 40 hours towards Yoga Alliance Continuing Education or towards 300 Hour Teaching Certification.


Healing Environment:


- Serene resort setting amidst Chiang Rai's cooling season

- Carefully curated vegetarian meals supporting mental clarity

- Individual and group meditation guidance


DAILY PROGRAMME


Your Daily Schedule looks like this:

6:00 am : Morning Meditation & Gentle Yoga

9:00 am : Breakfast

11:00 am : Afternoon Session

1:00 pm : Lunch & Rest / Self Practice

5:00 pm : Evening Yoga & Meditation

7:00 pm : Dinner


BOOK NOW

Secure your spot for this transformative journey of silence, self-discovery, and inner renewal. A profound experience of mindfulness awaits you. To signup for this course, please head over to Museflower Retreat to make your reservation and booking.
